2605.30284.title: ProjectionBench: Evaluating Scientific Hypothesis Generation in LLMs Under Progressive Information Disclosure
2605.30284.paper_summary: Scientific discovery is an inherently creative and uncertain process, requiring reasoning beyond the recall of known knowledge. While many benchmarks have been proposed to evaluate large language model (LLM) performance on deep research tasks via multi-hop retrieval, their innovative reasoning abilities essential for true scientific discovery remain largely untested. We introduce a benchmark framework for evaluating model performance in scientific discovery and reasoning, building up from a raw problem to the classical null hypothesis test. In our framework, models initially receive only the topic and research question from a recent paper, with technical details progressively revealed. At each stage of information disclosure, the model is tasked with generating hypotheses that address the research question, which is compared with the conclusions from the original paper and evaluated via automated semantic similarity of constituent atomic claims. This progressive evaluation of semantic divergence from ground-truth conclusions enables assessment of a model's innovativeness (under minimal information) to grounded reasoning capabilities (under full experimental details), both critical for using LLMs for scientific discovery purposes. Our framework provides a foundation for systematically evaluating scientific reasoning and discovery capabilities in LLMs, crucial for advancing the development of next-generation AI scientist/co-scientist systems. Specifically, here we evaluate GPT-5, GPT-5.4, Gemini 2.5 pro, and Gemini 3.1 pro preview across 45 papers spanning bioactive materials, mechanical materials, and nanomaterials. We find that GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3.1 pro outperform their previous generation counterparts as expected, and GPT-5.4 in particular maintains 0.7 F1 score alignment with ground truth conclusions even under minimal context.
2605.30284.paper_summary_zh: 科學發現本質上是一個創造性和不確定的過程，需要超越已知知識的推理。雖然已提出許多基準來評估大型語言模型（LLM）在多步檢索的深度研究任務上的表現，但它們在真實科學發現中所需的創新推理能力仍然大多未經測試。我們引入了一個基準框架，用於評估模型在科學發現和推理中的表現，從一個原始問題構建到經典的虛無假設檢驗。在我們的框架中，模型最初僅接收來自最近論文的主題和研究問題，技術細節逐步揭示。在每個信息披露階段，模型的任務是生成針對研究問題的假設，並將其與原始論文的結論進行比較，通過構成原子聲明的自動語義相似性進行評估。這種對語義偏差的逐步評估使得能夠評估模型的創新性（在最少信息下）與基於實驗細節的推理能力（在完整實驗細節下），這對於將LLM用於科學發現目的至關重要。我們的框架為系統評估LLM中的科學推理和發現能力提供了基礎，這對於推進下一代AI科學家/共同科學家系統的發展至關重要。具體而言，我們在45篇涵蓋生物活性材料、機械材料和納米材料的論文中評估了GPT-5、GPT-5.4、Gemini 2.5 pro和Gemini 3.1 pro預覽。我們發現GPT-5.4和Gemini 3.1 pro如預期地超越了其前一代對手，特別是GPT-5.4在最少上下文下仍保持與真實結論的0.7 F1分數對齊。
2605.30284.author: A. J. Lew et.al.
2605.30284.authors: A. J. Lew, Y. Cao, M. J. Buehler
